items.question: Which phrase best captures deep learning in this roadmap?
options: Any program that uses if-else rules
Multi-layer neural networks that learn representations from data
items.answer: Multi-layer neural networks that learn representations from data
items.explanation: Canonical DL here means stacked neural layers plus representation learning — not all AI, not only Gen AI.

items.question: Deep learning is a subset of:
items.explanation: AI ⊃ ML ⊃ DL. DL specializes within ML via deep neural networks.

items.question: Representation learning means:
options: Layers discover useful internal features during training
Copying weights from a random model
items.answer: Layers discover useful internal features during training
items.explanation: Hidden layers learn features tuned to the task instead of relying solely on engineered columns.

items.question: A common reason to prefer classical ML over DL on a tiny tabular dataset is:
options: Trees or linear models may generalize better with less data and compute
items.answer: Trees or linear models may generalize better with less data and compute
items.explanation: Small structured problems often favor interpretable classical models unless DL clearly wins on validation.

items.question: Modern generative AI systems are mostly built on:
items.explanation: Transformers, diffusion models, and similar architectures are deep networks trained for generation tasks.
